_comment: Default per-phase runtime+model+effort configuration. ``runtime`` selects a registered agent backend (claude / claude-glm / codex / gemini / plugin runtimes). ``effort`` (low|medium|high|xhigh|max) controls reasoning depth and is mapped to each runtime's CLI: Claude --effort, Codex -c model_reasoning_effort. Omit / null to let the CLI use its own default (e.g. ~/.codex/config.toml). Override locally via _config/config.local.json, ~/.orcho/config.local.json, $ORCHO_WORKSPACE/.orcho/config.local.json, or env vars (RUNTIME_<PHASE> / MODEL_<PHASE>).

timeouts._comment: Hard caps use *_seconds and are disabled by 0. Idle watchdogs use *_idle_seconds and reset whenever the agent streams output.

session._comment: Caller-side policy for implement → repair_changes session continuation (ADR 0023). 'mode' picks the SessionMode (auto/stateless/chain/hybrid); 'chain_same_model_only' guards CHAIN against accidental cross-model resume. AUTO also suppresses CHAIN when prior implement usage exceeds the token or tool-call budget. Runtimes may capture session_id on every invoke; this only controls when the orchestrator passes continue_session=True.

hypothesis._comment: Legacy direct-helper fallback only. Normal profile dispatch reads PhaseStep.hypothesis {attempts, format} from pipeline_profiles_v2.json.

pipeline._comment: Loop budgets (plan / repair) live in the active profile's LoopStep.max_rounds; per-phase pause semantics live in profile-declared handoff policies. change_handoff controls authoring→review hand-off mode (uncommitted | commit | commit_set). implementation_execution controls implement delivery (whole_plan | subtask_dag); semantic profile selection is normally explicit and may be delegated to the auto_detect selector. session_split_override may override profile-declared prompt-session split per phase for a workspace or one run.

auto_detect._comment: Selector-only auto-detect of the semantic profile + operating mode (Stage C). policy: confirm (TTY shows the recommendation for operator accept/override; non-TTY degrades to trusted threshold gating) | trust_above_threshold (deterministic auto-selection). confidence_threshold (0..1) is the trusted-accept bar. fallback_profile is a concrete SemanticProfile used when low-confidence/error falls back. on_low_confidence / on_error: fallback | fail. Manual concrete --profile never invokes the detector and never reads this section.

topology_signals._comment: Data-driven topology heuristic (Stage T1). Each phrase is lower-case substring-matched against the task text; a match recommends a cross topology over the union of the listed project aliases (orcho-core ordered first as primary). No model is involved. Override or extend per workspace.

cli._comment: Defaults for `orcho run` / `orcho-run` / `orcho-cross` CLI flags. output_mode: summary | live | debug — transcript verbosity used when neither --output nor its aliases (--stream-output / --verbose / -v) are passed. Override via ORCHO_OUTPUT_MODE env var.

accounting._comment: Dollar-denominated accounting is opt-in. When disabled, Orcho records token/time usage but does not store or render cost-reference fields. Enable locally with accounting.enabled=true or ORCHO_ACCOUNTING=1.

commit._comment: Post-release commit delivery. When enabled and the run-owned worktree has a diff after an APPROVED release verdict, Orcho can fix / approve / apply / skip / halt delivery. interactive_default is the preselected interactive action; auto_in_ci controls unattended runs. add_untracked controls delivery of non-ignored untracked files; approve also stages them. include_pre_existing_dirty and git_user_identity are reserved follow-up knobs from ADR 0032; Phase B1 keeps pre-existing dirty state user-owned and relies on the repo's configured git identity.

language._comment: Engine defaults stay English so cross-machine prompt rendering is reproducible. plan_language / task_language cover operator-facing artifacts; content_language covers outward delivery artifacts (commit message, PR title/body) and stays English as a fail-safe for public repositories regardless of the operator language. Workspaces whose team writes tasks / plans in another natural language should override here (or via PLAN_LANGUAGE / TASK_LANGUAGE / CONTENT_LANGUAGE env vars).

artifacts._comment: mirror_to_project: копировать ли семантические артефакты (план, diff, review, todo) из runspace/runs/<ts>/ в <project>/<mirror_dir>/ для git-трекинга. По умолчанию выкл — все артефакты живут в runspace.

worktree._comment: Per-run git worktree isolation (ADR 0033). When enabled, agent subprocesses run in an orcho-managed worktree under <workspace>/runspace/worktrees/<worktree_id>/checkout/ instead of the user's source checkout, so destructive git inside the worktree cannot harm user-owned uncommitted work. isolation: per_run (default) | off. retention_days: how long to keep the worktree on disk past run-end for inspection (orcho gc sweeps past TTL). allow_destructive_inside: reserved for future tightening; v1 always relaxes inside the worktree.

pre_run_dirty._comment: Pre-run dirty intake (ADR 0044). When enabled and per-run isolation would start from HEAD while the project checkout is dirty, interactive callers choose whether to include the current uncommitted diff, exclude it, commit first, or halt. interactive_default is the preselected interactive action; non_interactive_default controls unattended starts. include_untracked: prompt | all | none.

sandbox._comment: Launch-layer hygiene (ADR 0034). env allowlist + resource limits + token masking + child-process cleanup. mode: off|env (env is the default). env_allowlist appends to the built-in list (PATH/HOME/LANG/locale + provider API keys + ORCHO_* prefix); env_denylist always wins. limits use 0 to mean no limit. masking.builtin_patterns covers sk-ant-, sk-, AIza- token shapes; custom_patterns extends per-profile. Runtime sandbox (Claude Code / Codex etc.) covers file-access policy, tool-call approvals, and command guards — orcho does not duplicate them here.
